summary: APPROVE. All four prior contract findings are addressed by the normalize-at-source revision. (1) Point 4 genuinely adopted: a single shared helper resolves each Impacted-Domains entry to its owning domain NAME and is consumed by ALL THREE gates — bead-time divergence.go (~L142 candidateDomains) AND plan-time checkADRCoverage + checkADRCitations (both fed by loadImpactedDomains ~L124, now in In-Scope at L261-262). This closes my prior incomplete-fix coherence gap: the plan-time coverage/cite lane is now in scope, so a file-path Impacted-Domains spec passes divergence AND plan gates with no spurious adr-divergence-unowned / adr-coverage-missing / adr-cite-irrelevant. The L274 cross-gate-consistency claim is now actually supported by the In-Scope set. (2) Blast-radius guard explicitly PRESERVED, not silently dropped (R1 L167-174; AC L188-192; rejected-alt L374-377): attribution stays scoped to the normalized DECLARED set, and TestCompleteRejectsUndeclaredDomainTouch keeps its original adr-divergence-uncovered intent. Keeping it as the existing ERROR (rather than my suggested WARN) is coherent and intentional — my WARN suggestion was premised on the guard being dropped; since the revision keeps declared-set scoping, the status-quo ERROR is the correct choice and preserves the Spec 087 contract unchanged. (3) ADR-0032 tension properly resolved: the spec now lists ADR-0032 as an ADR it AMENDS (L117-129, L272-275, L412-418), with a concrete plan that softens sub-decision 1's outright rejection of path-like identifiers — path-like entries are NORMALIZED to the owning dir-name when exactly one domain's OWNERSHIP.yaml claims them, erroring on 0/>1 owners. This directly addresses sub-decision 1's 'path-like rejected as ambiguous' clause: ambiguity is resolved by owner-set cardinality rather than blanket rejection, and the canonical dir-name identifier is still what the gates compare (reached by resolution, not rejection). Mirrors the existing tri-state amendment precedent and spec 099's ADR-0037 amendment — sound. ADR-0036 is no longer over-claimed: it is now scoped to glob-matching against EXPLICIT paths: manifests with no synthesized fallback (L109-116, L389-393), which is genuinely ADR-0036's decision; the over-broad 'which domain SET to consult' claim is gone. (4) meta.Domains' load-bearing role is now accurate — the 'advisory' framing is removed; the field is characterized as live, name-matched input to all three gates that must be normalized at its source (Background L50-63, R1 Design L363-387), and entries are resolved to real domain names rather than demoted. No residual contract incoherence. Minor non-blocking note: helper placement (internal/validate vs internal/ownership) is left as a hedged plan-time detail (L98, L141-142) — both are workflow-owned, so it is not a contract gap. R2/R3/R4 contracts remain clean.
